abstract_ts: Some one-loop diagrams with one and two external baryons/nucleons are revisited using covariant baryon propagators in chiral effective theory. We showed that it is enough to separate and subtract all the local terms that violate chiral power counting to recover chiral power counting, no need to introduce extra operations. The structures of leading chiral corrections and IR enhancement or threshold effects are ‘stable’ or persist as long as covariant propagators are employed for all particles.

abstract_ts: In the field of object detection, existing multimodal fusion approaches have demonstrated the ability to effectively integrate frame-based and event-based data. However, they often neglect the crucial spatiotemporal characteristics inherently embedded within event streams. Furthermore, models frequently struggle to adaptively assess the relative importance of the two modalities under varying environmental conditions. To overcome these limitations, this study introduces two major contributions. First, we develop a Spatiotemporal Feature Encoder that thoroughly extracts and represents the key spatiotemporal information contained in event data. Second, we propose an enhanced fusion module, termed G-CAFR, built upon the CAFR framework. During the initial fusion stage, G-CAFR integrates a Noise-Modulated Dual Gating Mechanism, which introduces learnable noise into the softmax gating operation to achieve stochastic yet controllable weighting between RGB and event features. This mechanism facilitates the generation of more balanced and discriminative multimodal representations. Comprehensive experiments on the PKU-DDD17-Car dataset demonstrate that our method surpasses the state-of-the-art CAFR baseline by 2.3% in detection accuracy, while also exhibiting superior generalization capability and robustness across diverse scenarios.

abstract_ts: With the increase in the complexity of deep learning models, the need of energy-efficient hardware, in particular multiple multiply accumulate (MAC) units, has grown urgently. The present paper presents a multidimensional approximate MAC architecture that unites a number of approximation methods, such as truncated multiplication, Lower-Part OR Adder (LOA), Skip-Add and probabilistic gating in a single register-transfer level (RTL) of architecture. The design implements a core control strategy which seeks to configure approximation modes flexibly to trade off accuracy and energy savings according to task demands. The CIFAR-10 experiments demonstrate that the implemented design is capable of causing a maximum reduction of switching activity (86.5) with a tolerable accuracy in a conservative setting. More detailed analysis is done with Pareto frontiers and error quantiles. It indicates that in spite of ellagic approximation saving greater amounts of power, there are indeed instances of it causing significant errors. The architecture provides reconfigurable RTL implementations that can be helpful with deep learning accelerators, particularly with edge artificial intelligence (AI) applications.

abstract_ts: Cybersecurity has become one of the most pressing challenges in the digital era, as rapid technological advancements have also introduced new vulnerabilities, particularly in Europe, where cyber threats endanger both economic stability and national security. In order to mitigate these risks, the European Union has developed a comprehensive cybersecurity strategy, that incorporates legal frameworks, regulatory measures, and international cooperation to enhance resilience against cyberattacks. This study applies a quantitative methodology, using hierarchical clustering and Principal Component Analysis (PCA), to model cybersecurity resistance patterns among 44 European countries based on five dimensions of the Global Cybersecurity Index (GCI): Legal, Technical, Organizational, Capacity Development, and Cooperation measures. The analysis reveals clusters of countries based on cybersecurity maturity, exposing sociological and political patterns related to cyber resilience. The results reveal the leaders - and the laggards - when it comes to cybersecurity preparedness. They call for targeted capacity-building, coherent policies, and enhanced international cooperation to build a common and robust cybersecurity terrain throughout Europe. The findings have practical implications for policymakers, practitioners, and researchers who aim to address regional cybersecurity disparities and advance collaborative digital security efforts.

abstract_ts: This paper presents an enhanced model for IT project cost estimation based on the functional value method, which enables the determination of software volume from the perspective of its functionality for the end user. The methodology relies on analyzing the system’s logical functions and their complexity level, taking into account external factors, which ensures independence from specific technological implementations. The development process of IT projects involves iterative refinement of requirements, which introduces data uncertainty. A fuzzy system for evaluating software system complexity based on external factors has been constructed. The proposed model improves the accuracy of resource, timeline, and budget planning, and also contributes to the unification of evaluation criteria when comparing alternative solutions in the field of software development.

abstract_ts: Allomaternal care (AMC) is provided to the offspring by individuals other than the genetic mother, including several seemingly altruistic behaviors such as babysitting, carrying, nursing, crèching, or huddling for thermoregulation. To determine whether there is any allomaternal care among free-ranging domestic dogs, from March 2015 to May 2019 a total of eight dog groups consisting of 19 adult females (range 2–3 females per group) that were matrilineal relatives, i.e., maternal grandmothers, aunts, daughters, sisters were observed during pup-rearing period (1st to 13 weeks of pups’ life). During the first two weeks, lactating bitches were observed to spend the most time with their own pups and suckled for almost the entire observation period (30 min). Lactating mothers started to venture out of their dens for short foraging trips when the pups were 1 week old, but it was more frequent at 3 weeks of age. From the 3rd week of kin-related pups’ life the lactating mothers were observed to wean, with a parallel shift of nursing and regurgitation for the pups done by other kin-related females showing the evidence of allonursing. The occurrence of all types of nursing activities i.e., duration of contact, nursing, feeding by regurgitation, and protection of pups was higher among the mothers than among the allomothers. In the case of biological mothers, the frequency and duration of contact, nursing and feeding by regurgitation decreased as the puppies aged, meanwhile in the case of allomothers, the frequency and duration of contact, nursing and feeding by regurgitation increased until 6 weeks of age, before switching to a decreasing trend. Allonursing in free-ranging domestic dogs was more common among older bitches i.e., maternal grandmothers. We propose four possible reasons of allonursing in the case of free-ranging dogs- 1) having excess milk perhaps due to high pup mortality allonursing may evolve as low cost behavior in free-ranging dogs, 2) allonursing may provide substantial benefits to pups regarding growth, survival, and the transfer of immune compounds, 3) insufficiency of own mother’s milk may be one of the causes of allonursing, and 4) free-ranging domestic dogs nurse each other’s pups perhaps to promote a set of affiliating and permissive relationships among the group members. As the studied groups contained matrilineal related female dogs that helped each other with allonursing, this study strongly suggests the kin selection theory especially as allonursing is highly prevalent in other, closely related, gregarious wild canids (gray wolves, golden jackals)..

abstract_ts: In recent years, secure and efficient image data transmission in Internet of Things (IoT) systems has become increasingly important, especially in real-time video and image capture applications. This study proposes a novel blockchain-based architecture that enables secure image transmission from IoT devices using Hyperledger Besu and Python-based smart contracts. The proposed system consists of TurtleBot and Raspberry Pi for dynamic and static image capture, utilizes 128 KB chunking for efficient data segmentation, and ensures data integrity through SHA-256 hashing. The Hyperledger Besu network, configured with a QBFT (IBFT 2.0) consensus algorithm, guarantees trusted data logging across four nodes for each device. Experimental studies demonstrate the proposed system's reliability in preserving data integrity, reducing latency, and optimizing resource consumption. Also, performance and security analyses validate the system's applicability for real-time, privacy-sensitive IoT environments.

abstract_ts: Emotion recognition from audio signals is essential in forensic applications, offering insight into emotional states during interrogations, threat assessments, and crime scene analysis. This paper proposes an Inception-based deep learning model tailored for forensic arabic audio emotion recognition. The Inception architecture, with its multiscale feature extraction capabilities, efficiently captures subtle emotional details from complex audio signals. The model was evaluated on a dataset that represents a diverse range of emotional expressions, achieving superior performance in accuracy, robustness, and adaptability compared to traditional approaches. Its precision and ability to handle real-world variability make it particularly suited for forensic investigations. This work underscores the potential of advanced neural architectures in enhancing forensic decision-making and analysis.

abstract_ts: Multimodal learning is critical for robust perception in complex systems, yet integrating diverse modalities while ensuring fault tolerance remains a significant challenge. This paper presents a novel approach for fusing multimodal latent representations using a denoising variational autoencoder framework, where the fusion is achieved through the multiplication of probability density functions corresponding to each modality. By modeling each modality as a Gaussian distribution in the latent space, we derive a fused representation that optimally combines information from all modalities while preserving their probabilistic structure. We introduce a failure injection mechanism during training, where non-linear transformations simulate realistic faults in individual modalities. Experiments on industrial datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves superior reconstruction accuracy and robustness compared to existing methods, even in the presence of corrupted or missing modalities.

abstract_ts: Neural networks have emerged as a pivotal machine learning paradigm within contemporary artificial intelligence research, particularly for their adeptness at modeling complex input-output relationships. This paper addresses the training of shallow neural networks, focusing on optimizing the widely used Levenberg-Marquardt (LM) algorithm. While the LM algorithm facilitates faster convergence through adaptive learning rates and the use of Jacobian matrices, it also presents challenges in computational complexity and memory usage due to the growth of Jacobian matrices with network and dataset dimensions. To mitigate issues such as catastrophic forgetting and computational complexity and memory usage due to the growth of Jacobian matrices, this study proposes a Federated Multiple Dataset Levenberg-Marquardt (fmLM) algorithm. By employing distinct mask vectors for each dataset, the fmLM enables selective activation of network weights, allowing simultaneous training across multiple datasets while maintaining performance. Experimental results demonstrate that the fmLM algorithm achieves a promising error levels after training, effectively managing the balance between weight sharing and retention of prior learning. This approach not only enhances the efficiency of neural network training but also contributes a novel solution to catastrophic forgetting, underscoring the potential for improved generalization in neural networks.

abstract_ts: Reinforcement learning (RL) systems for autonomous driving require carefully designed simulation environments to facilitate training and testing in dynamic and complex scenarios. This paper presents the architecture of a simulation environment tailored to train autonomous vehicle decision-making systems, particularly for maneuver-based decisions such as Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) and Automatic Emergency Braking (AEB). We emphasize the integration of key components, including vehicle sensors, controllers, and environment models, with a specific focus on the real-time communication required to support RL training. Utilizing the Webots simulator and the ExerShuttle project as a case study, this paper demonstrates how RL-based agents can be integrated into a simulated driving environment, addressing challenges in data flow accuracy, system responsiveness, and component interoperability. The proposed architecture provides a scalable and flexible framework for developing robust RL models that adapt to diverse driving conditions, supporting the advancement of safe and efficient autonomous systems.

abstract_ts: Type 1 diabetes is a growing global health challenge. Standard clinical practice often relies on manual insulin injections, which can lead to suboptimal glucose regulation. Recent advancements have shifted focus towards Artificial Pancreas systems, integrating continuous glucose monitoring with automated insulin delivery. This work presents a quantitative comparison of four Deep Reinforcement Learning algorithms for autonomous glycemic regulation via insulin injection: DDPG, PPO, SAC, and TD3. The validation is conducted using the Hovorka model, in presence of uncertainties on number, time and amount of meals. Results show that all four controllers are able to maintain blood glucose levels within the target range. The TD3 algorithm outperforms the others in terms of several key performance indicators such as time in range, time in hypo/hyperglycemia and total insulin usage, while also exhibiting fewer hyperglycemic episodes compared to prior works in academic literature.

abstract_ts: Traffic light recognition is a critical component of autonomous driving and intelligent transportation systems, requiring accurate and efficient detection in real-time scenarios. In this study, BiFPN-YOLOv8 is used as a high-performance deep learning model for traffic light detection, which enhances YOLOv8n by incorporating the Bidirectional Feature Pyramid Network (BiFPN) and an additional detection head. While YOLOv8n is known for its speed and accuracy, its ability to detect small and distant objects is limited. By integrating BiFPN, the model improves multi-scale feature fusion, leading to enhanced detection of small traffic lights under various lighting and occlusion conditions. Additionally, the inclusion of a fourth detection head (P2 layer) further strengthens the recognition of distant traffic signals. We evaluate the proposed model using the LISA dataset, consisting of 14,034 daylight traffic light images across six distinct classes. Experimental results demonstrate that BiFPN-YOLOv8n achieves a mean average precision (mAP) of 94.19%, outperforming baseline models such as YOLOv8n (92.31%) and Tiny-YOLOv7-3L (90.35%). These improvements make BiFPN-YOLOv8 a promising solution for real-world traffic light detection and other vision tasks requiring precise multi-scale object recognition.

abstract_ts: Hybrid quantum-classical algorithms are the subject of intensive research in quantum computing and specifically in the domains of Machine Learning and Approximate Optimization. Simple distance-based learning classifiers such as kNN or k-means classifiers have already been the subject of many proposals, but most of their quantum implementation only consider a very small number of classes/clusters and only use metrics suitable for normalized unit length quantum data. Very few approaches combine the need for Euclidean distance of unnormalized data with an efficient quantum computation implementation. Based on two kNN and k-means proposals from the literature, this paper proposes their adaptation to the design of a Quantum Self-Organizing Map in order to solve the NP-hard Traveling Salesman Optimization Problem (TSP) specifically. Hence, we evaluate the hybrid quantum-classical approach to Euclidean TSP instances of sizes from 52 to 127 cities and present the properties, advantages, and limitations of the quantum implementation in solving the TSP optimization problem.

abstract_ts: Aiming at the problems of nonlinearity and uncertainty in transportation cyber-physical systems (TCPSs), this paper proposes an elastic control strategy based on interval type-2 (IT-2) fuzzy model for TCPSs with external disturbances. Firstly, considering the suspension system as a key link between the vehicle and the road, which is selected as the main object of study. According to IT-2 fuzzy rules, a fuzzy suspension system model is established to characterize its nonlinearity and uncertainty. Then, an elastic controller based on IT-2 model is established, and sufficient conditions are derived by Lyapunov function. Finally, the advancement and feasibility of the proposed theory are verified by simulation.

abstract_ts: With the increasing adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) in urban areas like Tunis, the strategic placement of charging stations is paramount. This paper presents a novel approach to optimize the placement of EV chargers in Tunis using a clustering-based bi-objective evolutionary algorithm. This algorithm simultaneously minimizes two objectives which may be conflicting: the costs of the chargers installation and the coverage of the charging infrastructure. By considering these objectives concurrently, the proposed method aims to strike a balance between minimizing the deployment costs and ensuring a sufficient coverage to meet the users demands. Through simulations conducted on a benchmark example in Tunis, we assess the algorithm’s effectiveness in generating Pareto-optimal solutions that offer trade-offs between cost and coverage. The findings underscore the potential of the bi-objective algorithm in proposing new interesting solutions for the placement of EV chargers, thereby contributing to the development of a sustainable transportation infrastructure in Tunis.

abstract_ts: Healthcare systems are essential to society but face numerous challenges in maintaining their resilience and long-term viability. Disruptions, much like those experienced in supply chains-can significantly hinder operational continuity. To mitigate such risks, strategic investments can be made to reduce the likelihood of service interruptions. However, given the constraints of a limited protection budget, it is crucial to allocate resources efficiently to enhance the resilience of selected critical facilities. The objective of this research is to develop an optimal protection strategy that minimizes both patient travel distances for accessing care and the number of patients left without timely treatment due to disruptions. Since accurately predicting facility failures remains difficult, robust optimization provides a valuable framework. In particular, a scenario-based robust decision-making approach allows for the evaluation of multiple plausible disruption scenarios, ensuring that the selected strategy performs well under various conditions. Our methodology leverages scenario analysis to identify the most effective investment strategy for sustaining healthcare system performance over time. Each scenario corresponds to a different allocation of protective investments, and we assess their respective impacts on system resilience. By analyzing the relationship between the fortification of specific facilities and overall system performance, we generate insights to inform budget allocation decisions aimed at maximizing resilience. This process helps quantify the relative importance of each facility, enabling an optimized distribution of resources within fixed budgetary limits.

abstract_ts: This paper investigates the implementation of feedback linearization for the control of a planar two-link robotic manipulator, with the objective of achieving precise and robust trajectory tracking. The nonlinear dynamic model of the manipulator is systematically derived using the Euler-Lagrange formalism, capturing the full complexity of its coupled dynamics. Based on this model, a nonlinear control law is designed to cancel the system’s intrinsic nonlinearities and impose a desired linear behavior.The proposed controller guarantees rapid convergence of tracking errors, stability of the closed-loop system, and robustness against modeling inaccuracies and dynamic interactions between the links. Through numerical simulations, the effectiveness of the approach is demonstrated, showing high tracking fidelity even in the presence of parameter uncertainties and external disturbances.

abstract_ts: Abstract-In this work, we present an innovative, simple, and accessible approach to capturing radio waves from the galactic continuum-ubiquitous on Earth-within a laboratory setting. The proposed solution allows students, educators, and enthusiasts-who may not have access to traditional radio telescopes-to engage with radio astronomy through a hands-on, cost-effective method. Unlike previous systems that require complex SDR programming or custom-built receivers, our design employs a basic λ/4 wire antenna connected to a reconfigurable spectrum analyzer-equipment commonly available in many teaching laboratories. This makes the setup immediately applicable for practical instruction.Our main contribution is to demonstrate that such a simplified configuration can reliably detect known signals from the galactic radio continuum. Measurements were conducted across several frequencies and compared with data from the literature, showing strong consistency and validating the system’s performance. The device functions as both a scientific instrument and an educational platform, helping democratize access to radio astronomy and fostering scientific curiosity in academic settings.By integrating all components into a unified laboratory environment (Radio Telescope-Lab), this work offers a reproducible and scalable solution that bridges the gap between advanced research instruments and accessible educational tools.

abstract_ts: We report a radical extension of magnetic-resonance spectroscopy to the Terahertz (THz) spectral range by complete polarization-resolved measurement of the linear electromagnetic response of nuclear and electron spin associated magnetic resonance using principles of ellipsometry at high magnetic fields. This new method permits us to accurately test existing models and leads to discovery of new physics in magnetic resonance such as the dynamic Bloch susceptibility model at THz frequencies, the magnetic Lyddane-Sachs-Teller relationship, the existence of longitudinal magnetic resonance modes, the temperature-dependence of the Brillouin magnetization, and rules of non-reciprocal sum and super convergence in dielectric, magnetic, and magnetoelectric spectral response dyadics. Our new method evolves from the previously reported Terahertz Optical Hall effect where measurements of free charge carriers under strong external magnetic fields provide access to critical band structure properties of semiconductors. Our new method provides sensitive information about quantum properties of defect and dopants, demonstrated for key semiconductors such as SiC, GaN, and Ga<inf>2</inf>O<inf>3</inf>, but is also broadly applicable to magnetic and ferromagnetic resonances, and more.

abstract_ts: In recent years, antenna-coupled field-effect transistors for THz detection (TeraFETs) have been established as fast and sensitive room-temperature detectors useable throughout the entire terahertz range (100 GHz to 10 THz). In sensing applications of quantum cascade lasers (QCLs), they are being employed as power detectors and have recently been established as sensors for direct-absorption gas spectroscopy. While single rectifying TeraFETs provide high sensitivity under optimized power coupling conditions, their optical sensitivity can suffer due to their small active sensing area if the radiation is not in-coupled properly. In addition, their response speed is reduced due their relatively high impedance when included into high-bandwidth readout circuits. A solution to overcome both of these limitations is to connect multiple detectors in parallel to form a single effective detector (pixel-binning). In this contribution, we directly compare the responsivity of three detectors to QCL radiation at 4.7 THz: a single-TeraFET detector and two multi-element ones having 4×4 pixels, respectively 8×8 pixels. All device are made from equally designed TeraFETs.

abstract_ts: The manipulation and detection of orbital angular momentum (OAM) in the terahertz (THz) regime offer promising advancements in applications such as communications and imaging. This work introduces a novel terahertz diffractive optical network capable of detecting OAM states, including individual states from 1 to 9, as well as 36 unique superposed combinations of any two OAM states. By utilizing an inverse designed 5-layer diffractive optical network, a precise spatial mapping of each OAM state and their combinations onto distinct spots on a THz camera is achieved. The network is fabricated with low-cost and low-loss high-impact polystyrene (HIPS) 3D printing material, offering a practical solution for scalable deployment. Experimental characterization is conducted by utilizing a 0.3 THz transmitter and an imaging camera, which confirms the effective practical performance of the DNN. The proposed approach provides a cost-effective and high-performance solution for THz OAM detection and paves the way for further advancements in THz-based applications.

abstract_ts: Nanoscale thermal imaging is essential for understanding energy transport and dissipation in advanced materials and devices. However, conventional infrared and terahertz imaging techniques are fundamentally diffraction-limited to the micrometer scale, making them unsuitable for probing nanoscale thermal dynamics. Previously, we developed near-field terahertz scanning noise microscopy (SNoiM) to image hot electrons at room temperature by detecting ultrahigh-frequency current fluctuations as a direct probe of nonequilibrium electrons. Here, we present Cryo-SNoiM, an advanced cryogenic version designed for quantitative hot electron temperature mapping at low temperatures. This instrumental breakthrough provides unprecedented insights into nonequilibrium electron dynamics and low-temperature transport phenomena, opening new avenues for studying quantum materials and nanoscale electronic systems.

abstract_ts: Scattering in water-paraffin oil emulsions was studied by terahertz time-domain spectroscopy (THz-TDS) with the aim of estimating droplet size and concentration. The loss coefficients in water-oil emulsion systems were measured at very low concentrations of water (≤ 1% v/v) to establish the sensitivity of THz-TDS. As expected, the scattering losses increased linearly with water concentration. The scattering spectra exhibited a quadratic dependence on frequency, indicating that Mie scattering was the dominant scattering loss mechanism in this system.

abstract_ts: We present novels methods aiming at recording THz waveforms in single-shot and high repetition rates, with high temporal resolution. This work is performed in the PhLAM-DESY collaboration framework, aiming at developing real-time diagnostics for THz sources, as well as monitoring the shapes of relativistic electron bunches in accelerators. While electro-optic probing with chirped laser probes is efficient for high repetition rate operations, it suffers from resolution and fidelity limitations, particularly for extended recording windows. To address these issues, we explore solutions based on the diversity concept, employing a multi-output electro-optic measurement system. This approach, combined with advanced reconstruction algorithms, enables faithful retrieval of the measured electric field. We have developed a new algorithm that "adapts" to laser chirp imperfections (high-order dispersion), enabling high-resolution single-shot broadband THz measurements over a long temporal window. We present the method's principle, numerical results, and the status of experimental developments at European XFEL and FLASH, demonstrating a significant advancement in high-resolution, long-duration of broadband THz characterization.

abstract_ts: In recent years, the phenomenon of “flash crowds"where access to specific websites or content surges suddenly and intensely-has been frequently reported. Such events can lead to server overloads and system failures, driven by degraded user experience and repeated retransmissions. Prompt and effective countermeasures are therefore essential. To address this issue, a load-balancing method called the Diffusion Control (DC) method, based on the diffusion equation on a network, has been proposed. However, prior studies have evaluated this method only under limited conditions, involving a small number of load balancers and symmetric network topologies, making it difficult to assess its practical advantages over alternative approaches. In this paper, we evaluate the DC method in comparison to a non-cooperative load-balancing method-where load balancers operate indepen-dently-within asymmetric network models. Through extensive simulations, we demonstrate that the DC method consistently provides more stable request-response behavior under flash crowd scenarios, highlighting its effectiveness in more realistic and asymmetric network topologies.

abstract_ts: An autonomous mobile robot system consists of multiple simple robots that are anonymous, identical, oblivious, and execute the same algorithm. Each robot repeatedly performs a Look-Compute-Movecycle, and clarifying the solvability of tasks under such restrictions is a central issue. The defected view model, introduced by Kim et al., captures faults in the Look operation, where a robot may fail to observe some others. Variants include distance-based, adversarial, and relaxed models. Previous work showed that gathering is possible in the distance-based (4,2)-defected view model under a fully synchronous scheduler, but its solvability under weaker schedulers remained open. This paper proves the impossibility of gathering problem in the distance-based $(4,2)$-defected view model even under a semi-synchronous scheduler, and further establishes the unsolvability of the distinct gathering problem in the same setting.

abstract_ts: Depression is one of the most serious medical problems. The World Health Organization (WHO) estimates that depression affects more than 264 million people worldwide, in all age groups. Depression is a major contributing factor to suicide. Alarming the urgent need for effective methods to detect and treat depression.In this paper, we conduct a comparative study of different situations applying general and specific domain language models. Specifically, we consider, among others, BERT, BioBERT, and ClinicalBERT using social media texts and medical notes as data sources. The results show that the models work better when the data sources are close to their training domain, but performance decreases when they are older or come from a different context. These findings highlight the importance of updating and adapting models in the field of mental health.

abstract_ts: This paper presents predictive analytics for chronic kidney disease (CKD) based on clinical features using a powerful deep learning-based model. We tested performance of four existing best deep learning algorithms, convolutional neural network (CNN), recurrent neural network (RNN), multilayer perceptron (MLP), and long short-term memory (LSTM). Our research, based on a large set of pre-processed clinical variables, has indicated that MLP model had a superb testing accuracy of 93.94%, thus it is the most accurate model. Interpretability of model was enhanced by the use of SHapley Additive exPlanation (SHAP) which is an interpretable machine learning methodology. TimeToEventMonths, eGFRBase-line, ACEIARB, CreatnineBaseline and HbA1C were the most significant clinical factors used to predict the model, based on SHAP analysis. The interpretability created by SHAP substantially enhances clinical decision-making by offering a reasonable and coherent interpretation of results projected. The findings provide additional evidence that interpretable methods, combined with advanced deep learning approaches, can contribute to the prediction of CKD.

abstract_ts: Attacks on the operating system (OS) kernel exploit vulnerable kernel functions via system call invocations. When investigating attacks on the kernel, it is necessary to identify vulnerable kernel functions. In analysis using eBPF or GDB, the kernel function name and virtual address are required for kernel investigation. However, it is difficult to manually identify vulnerable kernel function names and virtual addresses used in an attack for a large-scale OS such as Linux. In this study, we propose vulnerable kernel function flow (vkFlow) to automatically identify kernel functions and virtual addresses related to kernel functions of system call that are called during an attack. In the vkFlow, the kernel source code is statically analyzed, a function call graph is generated starting from the system call invocation, and the kernel function names are automatically identified. In addition, the kernel debug information and symbol information are referenced to automatically identify virtual address from the kernel function name. We applied vkFlow to Linux and evaluated whether it is possible to automatically identify vulnerable kernel functions and virtual addresses among kernel functions related to system calls. We also measured the calculation time required for kernel analysis using the vkFlow.

abstract_ts: The rapid rise of fake news and misinformation, especially on social media, has led to a surge of interest in developing ways to detect it. This bibliometric study takes a close look at 1625 publications on fake news detection from 2017 to 2025, drawing data from Scopus, Web of Science, and Google Scholar. We utilized statistical analysis and visualization tools of Python and VOSviewer to explore trends in publications, identify leading authors and institutions, examine international collaborations, and uncover thematic patterns. Our results indicate a steady increase in research output, with India emerging as the most productive country, while the UK and US lead in citation impact. Keyword clustering points to three primary research directions: advanced models such as deep learning and transformers, socially relevant issues like COVID-19 and hate speech, and traditional machine learning approaches. This study brings fresh insights by updating earlier analyses with a focus on NLP and open 2025 dataset. However, despite these advancements, collaboration networks are still quite limited, and the field predominantly relies on English-language sources. This study outlines the landscape of the field and suggests future paths for more inclusive, multilingual, and interdisciplinary research in fake news detection.

abstract_ts: Background: The current COVID-19 pandemic is associated with a variety of stressors. Preliminary research has demonstrated that general public are experiencing a range of psychological problems, including stress-related disturbances. However, to date, there is not much research on the prevalence of adjustment disorder during the current pandemic. Objectives: This study aimed to assess the prevalence and severity of symptoms of adjustment disorder compared to posttraumatic symptoms, depression and generalized anxiety in a large sample of adult Poles, in the first phase of the current pandemic. Method: Self-report data from a web-based sample (N = 1,742) was collected between March 25 and April 27, just after the introduction of nationwide quarantine measures in Poland. Results: The current COVID-19 pandemic was a highly stressful event for 75% of participants and the strongest predictor of adjustment disorder. Increased symptoms of adjustment disorder were reported by 49%, and they were associated with female gender and not having a full-time job. However, after exclusion of co-occurring symptomatology, 14% of the sample were finally qualified as meeting diagnostic criteria of adjustment disorder. A substantial proportion of the sample screened also positive for generalized anxiety (44%) and depression (26%); the rate for presumptive PTSD diagnosis was 2.4%. Conclusions: High rates of negative mental health outcomes were found in the Polish population in the first weeks into the COVID-19 pandemic and lockdown measures. They indicate the intense current stress-related symptoms in the early phase of the pandemic and warrant further monitoring on population's mental health.

abstract_ts: We investigated rain-induced crosstalk in THz parallel fixed wireless systems. Spatially separated narrow beams offer increased capacity via spatial division multiplexing, but rainfall causes scattering that induces crosstalk between channels. Using a 300 GHz outdoor transmission system and a controlled rainfall facility, we have successfully measured off-axis rain scattering.

abstract_ts: Here, we propose and experimentally verify highly efficient all-dielectric wavefront shaping devices with simultaneous polarization selectivity in the terahertz band. The molecular unit of the metasurface consists of a pair of subwavelength high-resistivity silicon pillars. By reasonably designing the geometric sizes and orientation angles of the silicon pillars, arbitrarily polarized terahertz wave can be obtained. At the designed operation frequency of 1THz, the power ratio of the metasurface polarizer is around 97%. Compared with the traditional terahertz polarizer implementation method, the selective transmission of arbitrary polarization is achieved only through a single-layer all-dielectric structure. Further studies show that by adjusting the geometric parameters of the two silicon pillars in the cell structure, continuous 2π phase coverage can be achieved, enabling functional integration of polarization selection and wavefront shaping. The monolithic integration of polarization generation and phase modulation achieved in this study has great potential application value in ultra-compact optical systems.

abstract_ts: The co-expression network of long non-coding RNA ROR (lncRNA-ROR) and microRNA-185-3p (miR-185-3p) has not been focused on osteosarcoma. Therein, this work was initiated to uncover lncRNA-ROR and miR-185-3p functions in osteosarcoma. LncRNA-ROR, miR-185-3p and Yes-associated protein 1 (YAP1) expression in osteosarcoma tissues and cells were detected. The screened cells (MG63 and U2OS) were transfected with decreased and/or increased lncRNA-ROR and miR-185-3p to explore osteosarcoma progression. Tumor growth was detected by tumor xenografts in mice. Up-regulated lncRNA-ROR and YAP1 and down-regulated miR-185-3p were found in osteosarcoma. LncRNA ROR knockdown or miR-185-3p overexpression inhibited osteosarcoma cell progression while lncRNA ROR elevation or miR-185-3p inhibition presented the opposite effects. Function of lncRNA ROR was rescued by miR-185-3p and regulated the growth and metastasis of osteosarcoma cells via modulating YAP1, the target gene of miR-185-3p. This work illustrates that lncRNA-ROR down-regulation or miR-185-3p up-regulation inhibits osteosarcoma progression via YAP1 repression.

abstract_ts: This study proposes a new Enhanced Lion Swarm Optimization Algorithm with Centralized Authentication (ELSOA-CA) to improve the security of data transmission in WSNs. By inspecting energy, delay and link quality factors, the proposed ELSOA-CA approach can select a better forwarder using methods inspired by lions in nature. When compared with ACO and LSDAR, the study finds that the simulation results reveal major improvements. The calculated method reduces end-to-end delay to 0.01 seconds which is less than the 0.31 seconds achieved by ACO. Throughput increases to 0.75 packets/sec, higher than that seen in LSDAR at 0.74 and ACO at 0.66. The typical approach requires 0.08 J of energy, while the process we used only needs 0.001 J. Not only that, but ELSOA-CA is able to keep the network operating for 4.001 seconds which is much longer than the 0.8 seconds of ACO and the 1.8 of LSDAR. A drop rate of 0.86 % means that reliability is improved when compared to before. ELSOA-CA provides strong, efficient and secure multipath routing which is suitable for tracking tomato crop growth in a greenhouse.

abstract_ts: The proliferation of Android-based applications as primary access points to e-governance platforms has intensified concerns regarding malware that threatens citizen privacy, service integrity, and national digital infrastructure. While deep learning (DL) techniques have demonstrated significant promise in Android malware detection, their performance is limited by sensitivity to hyperparameter tuning, redundant features, and imbalanced datasets. This paper proposes a novel Chameleon Swarm Algorithm (CSA)optimized deep learning framework for robust Android malware detection in e-governance environments. Inspired by the adaptive hunting strategies of chameleons, CSA balances exploration and exploitation to optimize feature subset selection, hyperparameter tuning, and class-weight calibration in deep neural architectures. The framework integrates static (permissions, API calls, opcodes) and dynamic (network traffic, system calls) features into a hybrid CNN-BiLSTM model, with CSA performing meta-heuristic optimization. Experiments conducted on benchmark datasets (Drebin, CICMalDroid 2020, AndroZoo) demonstrate that the CSA-optimized model outperforms state-of-the-art approaches-including PSO, GA, Grey Wolf Optimizer, and Arithmetic Optimization Algorithm-across metrics such as F1-score, AUC, and MCC. The framework achieves 97.8 % accuracy with a 2.4 % reduction in false positives, highlighting its effectiveness in protecting citizen data and ensuring the trustworthiness of Android-based e-governance services. Beyond improved detection, the system offers feature interpretability, energy efficiency, and adaptability for real-world deployment, contributing toward the vision of a resilient Cyber Surakshit Bharat.

abstract_ts: Since parking allocation being one of the main challenges in modern cities, the need for a smart parking system has emerged. This paper carries out an in-depth study, comparison, and analysis of various SPS that focus on the technological approaches, sensors, networking technologies, user interfaces, computational methodologies, and the range of services they offer. In order to address the critical issue of locating available parking spaces, we present a novel automated parking management system. The system ensures seamless communication through automated WhatsApp receipts with the help of the Twilio API, supports data backup at entry, exit, and logout, and offers a “Find My Vehicle” feature as well. It features user-friendly access, real-time tracking, and billing through cloud integration, and QR code-based parking. This is a system that leverages such advancements to improve operational efficiency, better resource utilization, and better user experience in addressing parking challenges in metropolitan areas.

abstract_ts: Port operations in India are becoming increasingly complicated as vessel arrivals, cargoes, and weather conditions change, necessitating the use of data-driven decision-making to enhance efficiency. Existing systems based on rule-based logic and disconnected deep learning models suffer from irrelevant, unchangeable thresholds, poor explainability, and isolated data connections, resulting in inaccurate stay time and berth assignment. The proposed machine learning-based predictive analysis platform uses AIS, manifest data, meteorological inputs, and operational logs to estimate vessel berth usage, delay class, and berth productivity. Using 2019-2024 Indian port datasets for training, it achieves an MAE of 4.1 hours, an 18% improvement over the old system, increases the delay classification F1-score from 0.68 to 0.79, and provides a 9.4% gain in berth productivity. These advantages include more accurate prediction, increased decisionmaking transparency, and quantifiable operational efficiency gains in various maritime conditions.

abstract_ts: Although research on bilingualism has attracted great scientific interest in recent decades, we still do not fully understand how bilinguals' language experience impacts language access and cognitive functioning. Our goal is to demonstrate that being exposed to one language, even for a short time, can influence the ability to use the other language and also affect how efficiently we process non-linguistic information. In this chapter, we focus on two types of effects related to the prior language experience of bilinguals (so-called language after-effects): (1) the impact of exposure to the second language on native language processing; (2) the impact of bilinguals' patterns of language use on cognitive control. For each topic, we review the available behavioral and neuropsychological evidence and discuss the possible sources of inconsistencies in the literature. Furthermore, we consider the implications of the available evidence for the current models of language “after-effects” and speculate about the possible convergence of mechanisms related to short- and long-term language experience..

abstract_ts: Employee sentiment, one of the most important drivers of retention, productivity, and organizational strategy, remains constrained by poor contextual understanding, low adaptability, and reliance on external or survey-only data sources via traditional HR sentiment systems employing lexicons or shallow classifiers. It becomes even more complex in organizations, where employee feedback may come from multiple channels, such as emails, helpdesk logs, and surveys, and must be unified using a deep learning-based sentiment analysis framework that acts as a preprocessor, combining domain-adaptive transformer embeddings, BiLSTM-CRF sequence tagging, and calibrated classification to provide the final sentiment score. It must utilize an even more complicated architecture that incorporates delicate language, privacy compliance, and increased capabilities for aspect-level sentiment detection. By testing on 300,000 anonymous records, it demonstrates that the proposed system reaches 92.1% accuracy, 0.947 AUROC, 0.910 F1-score, 5.1% calibration error, and 85% Confirm improved accuracy, robustness, and actionability to assist organizations in making credible, datadriven strategic HR choices.

abstract_ts: Nuclear medicine has an important role in the management of neoplastic and nonneoplastic bone diseases. abstract_ts: The primary objective of the study is to ascertain the potential integration of green value-chain methodologies and circular economy (CE) principles to mitigate the environmental impact of cost management. We use a design-oriented approach, case studies from diverse disciplines, and a comprehensive analysis of the literature to develop a framework that works. This theory suggests that money is involved in eco-designing products, digital traceability, and reverse logistics. The principle underlying this framework is “design-oriented methodology.” Organizations may become more eco-efficient by using less energy and materials if they make adjustments to their value chain that are in accordance with CE. They could also be able to improve their finance-related KPIs by making sure that their efforts are the proper magnitude. This will shorten the time it takes to pay back the loan and give you a favorable net present value. Closed-loop and refurbishment operations can help reduce waste. The results suggest that these companies can make these changes. The management framework divides the supply chain into five parts: supply-side, production, distribution, consumption, and disposal. During certain periods, you might be able to receive prizes.

abstract_ts: As a result of our research, we provide an AIbased bioinformatics framework. The system's purpose is to improve precision medicine by combining omics data with machine learning models and ensemble optimization methods. The suggested Bioformer-Hybrid method uses pipelines for dimensionality reduction, normalization, and feature augmentation to look at biological data before using transformer-based learning to make clinical predictions. The model does better than its rivals, with results like 0.94 [0.93-0.95], 0.92[0.90-0.93], an F1-score of 0.89, and 0.90 for sensitivity and 0.88 for specificity. These results are far better than those of well-known models like FT-Transformer and GraphSAGE. The model underwent a thorough review due to the integration of genomic, proteomic, and clinical data. This approach may be used on a wide range of people, including those of different ages, genders, and places, because it has a consistently high AUROC performance (0.91-0.93). As a consequence, people from all walks of life may benefit from it. The model is a great choice for therapeutic applications that are utilized in the real world since it offers many useful features, such as being easy to understand, having a small file size, and being able to be used in many different ways. These traits may help with many parts of healthcare, including making sure diagnoses are correct, tailoring therapy to each patient, and making decisions.

abstract_ts: LifeLane is a smart traffic clearance system designed to expedite emergency vehicle transit. It employs a dual-layer software architecture for real-time traffic signal preemption. Layer 1 uses Wi-Fi Direct or ESP-NOW for direct communication from an ambulance's ESP32 microcontroller to traffic signals, enabling immediate green light override. Layer 2, a fallback, streams GPS, speed, and heading data from the ESP32 to a Raspberry Pi at traffic junctions. Random Forest classifier and Hidden Markov Model (HMM) are employed to predict the ambulance's path, proactively preempting signals. All computations occur at the edge, ensuring rapid response and resilience without cloud dependency. The proposed model is compared with the existing system and its effectiveness is evaluated through experimental studies.

abstract_ts: This study shows how to use machine learning to improve business processes utilizing a multi-stage ensemble modeling framework. Sigma-enhanced stepwise random forest models, gradient-based residual refinement, and adaptive weak learner updates generate accurate, resilient, and understandable predictions in the study. Initial input aspects are identical. Bootstrapping is used to generate multiple decision trees to ensure model generalization and minimize overfitting. Each tree's predictions are combined to form a powerful ensemble, and hyperparameters are adjusted for optimal outcomes. In the second step, adaptive gradient-based algorithms repeatedly modify residuals. This procedure trains weak learners about prediction mistakes to address problems and stabilize the model. Residual optimization and varianceaware fine-tuning give low-error data more weight and recalibrate predictions for accurate, reliable results. When tested on uncertainty, feature contribution, classification, and regression, the suggested method always outperforms Decision Tree Classifier, Support Vector Machine, K-Nearest Neighbors, Gradient Boosting Regressor, and Adaptive Neural Network Ensemble. Low error metrics, forecast uncertainty, and good accuracy, precision, recall, <tex>$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score, <tex>$\mathbf{R}$</tex>-squared, and AUC values make the approach effective. The methodology generates actionable insights that simplify and scale business process optimization through feature importance analysis. You may create a robust, flexible, and trustworthy predictive system to assist you in making strategic and operational business decisions in adverse conditions using this strategy.

abstract_ts: Stem cells offer an array of advantages over conventional drug therapies since they are inherently biomimetic and offer numerous beneficial mechanisms and factors involved in tissue repair. Stem cells, particularly mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) have emerged as promising therapeutics for salivary gland regeneration owing to their higher regenerative potential and less immunogenicity. Over the past 10 years, due to the success of several pre-clinical studies, including modifications of somatic cells to have controlled potency of stemness and tissue regeneration, these findings have progressed to clinical trials. Numerous studies using pre-clinical animal models show MSCs as an effective treatment modality for salivary gland dysfunctions. In this chapter, we cover the recent pre-clinical and clinical studies involving adult MSCs for salivary gland regeneration conducted from 2015 to 2025.

abstract_ts: The convergence of Internet of Things (IoT) and Machine Learning (ML) paradigms is poised to revolutionize agri-business by transitioning from traditional practices to data-driven, intelligent farming. This paper proposes a novel integrated framework that leverages a distributed sensor network for real-time, in-situ monitoring of critical agronomic parameters, including soil moisture, nutrient levels, microclimate, and crop health. The voluminous, multi-modal data streams generated by these IoT devices are processed and fed into a predictive ML model, architected for robust crop yield forecasting. The model employs ensemble learning techniques to analyze historical and real-time data, accounting for spatial and temporal variability to generate accurate, high-resolution predictions. The proposed framework is designed to empower stakeholders with actionable intelligence for precise resource allocation, optimized irrigation and fertilization, early disease detection, and informed market decisions. By enhancing operational efficiency, mitigating risks, and maximizing profitability, this IoT and ML synergy establishes a new benchmark for sustainable and economically viable precision agri-business, ultimately contributing to global food security.

abstract_ts: This chapter focuses on the topic of Dental Age Estimation (DAE), which is primarily used in forensic age evaluation in the absence of any age record or where the age is purposefully hidden. It describes the fundamental concepts, including chronological, skeletal, and dental age. It highlights the significance of teeth as a biological characteristic of identity due to their development, growth, and resilience to environmental deterioration. This also depicts the extent of DAE in civil and humanitarian assistance works such as investigating asylee cases, criminal cases, and disaster victim identification. Indicated methods of visual, radiological, and biochemical DAE are elaborated upon for different age groups. New advances in 3D imaging like CBCT and micro-CT, and the recently introduced artificial intelligence technologies, are said to improve the objectivity and accuracy of results. This information sets the stage for a detailed discussion in the following chapters.

abstract_ts: Background: Large language models (LLMs) have greatly improved the accuracy of automated program repair (APR) methods. However, LLMs are constrained by high computational resource requirements. Aims: We focus on small language models (SLMs), which perform well even with limited computational resources compared to LLMs. We aim to evaluate whether SLMs can achieve competitive performance in APR tasks. Method: We conducted experiments on the QuixBugs benchmark to compare the bug-fixing accuracy of SLMs and LLMs. We also analyzed the impact of int8 quantization on APR performance. Results: The latest SLMs can fix bugs as accurately as-or even more accurately than-LLMs. Also, int8 quantization had minimal effect on APR accuracy while significantly reducing memory requirements. Conclusions: SLMs present a viable alternative to LLMs for APR, offering competitive accuracy with lower computational costs, and quantization can further enhance their efficiency without compromising effectiveness.

abstract_ts: Background: Identifier names, which comprise a significant portion of the codebase, are the cornerstone of effective program comprehension. However, research has shown that poorly chosen names can significantly increase cognitive load and hinder collaboration. Even names that appear readable in isolation may lead to misunderstandings in contexts when they closely resemble other names in either structure or functionality. Aims: This exploratory study aims to investigate the occurrence of similar identifier names in projects and develop a taxonomy categorizing name similarities. Method: Five open-source Java projects were analyzed using automated extraction and manual review. Results: Our findings reveal a taxonomy comprising seven categories of identifier name similarities. Conclusions: We envision our initial taxonomy providing researchers with a platform to analyze and evaluate the impact of identifier name similarity on code comprehension, maintainability, and collaboration among developers, while also allowing for further refinement and expansion of the taxonomy.

abstract_ts: The message routing protocol is essential for the efficient delivery of messages in Delay/Disruption-tolerant networks (DTNs). Messages dispatched by a mobile node are transmitted in a Store-Carry-Forward fashion between mobile nodes in Delay Tolerant Networks (DTNs). Current protocols comprise the behavior of mobile nodes, connection data, and movement models to enhance their mechanisms for message exchange during encounters. PRoPHET is one of the protocols attracting attention. It computes the likelihood of encountering other nodes and the message’s destination to regulate message forwarding. When the probability of an encounter diverges from the actual encounter, it cannot transmit messages effectively. We propose LH-PRoPHET, an enhancement of PRoPHET, that not only utilizes the encounter frequency for controlling message forwarding, but also takes into account the trajectory prediction based on the position history of encountered nodes. This approach seeks to maximize node coverage by transferring messages to nodes moving in divergent directions, hence enhancing the delivery ratio without increasing resource expenditure. Our simulation findings indicate that LH-PRoPHET markedly enhances the delivery ratio and latency while concurrently decreasing resource consumption.

abstract_ts: Cross-platform recommendation in digital health aims to break health data barriers and enable personalized health management, yet faces challenges like sparse electronic health records (EHRs), medical data heterogeneity, and privacy regulations (e.g., HIPAA). Existing methods mostly focus on algorithm optimization, lacking systematic privacy solutions for cross-institutional healthcare collaboration. This paper proposes DKD-ZKFL, integrating diffusion models, knowledge distillation (KD), and blockchain privacy tech (ZKP, FL) to build a "data-available-yet-invisible, model-trustworthy-and-verifiable" system. The diffusion model learns source-domain sparse data distributions via noise injection-denoising to generate target-domain augmented samples; KD transfers cross-domain knowledge between source-domain teacher and target-domain student models using soft/hard label supervision. By keeping raw data local, and combining ZKP dynamic sharding with FL gradient aggregation, it balances privacy and modeling efficiency, providing compliant solutions for digital health. Experiments demonstrate better recommendation accuracy and privacy security than traditional methods.

abstract_ts: This paper presents a data-driven, machine- learning framework to optimize the allocation of consumable firefighting equipment at the grassroots level. By leveraging historical incident data, unit-specific parameters, and intrinsic equipment covariates, we formulate a supervised regression model to predict two-year cumulative attrition volumes for various equipment categories. The proposed model derives evidence-based allocation standards, transitioning from coarse-grained, experience-based procurement to precise, predictive resource optimization. Descriptive and visual analytics reveal significant geographical imbalances and misalignments between current provisioning strategies and actual operational demand. Among six evaluated algorithms, Bayesian Ridge Regression (BYS) and Ridge Regression (RID) achieve the lowest mean squared error, outperforming linear, ensemble, and non-parametric alternatives. The framework reduces fiscal waste, mitigates premature equipment degradation, and enhances operational readiness. Future work will extend the model to non-consumable assets, integrate real-time IoT maintenance data, and validate its applicability across diverse jurisdictional contexts.

abstract_ts: This study develops a disaster-adaptive framework for modeling and simulating last-mile delivery feasibility under adverse weather conditions, specifically floods and landslides in rural settings. The model employs a parallelized Monte Carlo simulation governed by stochastic rainfall intensities, where disruptions are represented by dynamic adjustments to road network edge weights reflecting disaster-induced delays and inaccessibility. Delivery routes are recalculated iteratively using an adapted Nearest Neighbor Algorithm to incorporate evolving mobility constraints. To address the computational demands of large-scale stochastic modeling, the framework leverages Python’s multiprocessing capabilities, achieving a 3.36× speedup (69% of the theoretical maximum) compared to serial execution. This parallelization enables simultaneous simulation of multiple severity levels, enhancing scalability and responsiveness. The resulting model systematically evaluates delivery network performance under disaster conditions, providing predictive insights into adaptability, route viability, and resilience. By explicitly incorporating flood and landslide risks into rural logistics modeling and demonstrating the efficiency gains of parallel execution, this work advances both methodological and practical contributions to disaster-resilient logistics planning. It further supports the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation, and Infrastructure), SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities), and SDG 13 (Climate Action)

abstract_ts: Differential Privacy (DP) provides a rigorous framework for developing privacy-preserving applications. For discrete selection problems, the Exponential Mechanism (EM) offers a computationally tractable solution. However, the conventional EM fails to incorporate critical real-world constraints, such as non-uniform priors on the input domain or desired marginal distributions (evidence) over the output space. This limitation is significant, as the only alternative — direct optimization under such constraints — is often computationally prohibitive for large-scale applications, impeding the practical deployment of DP. To address this challenge, we introduce a novel utility formulation for the EM that explicitly models datapoints’ dissimilarity while integrating prior information. This formulation is parameterized, allowing us to generate an ensemble of candidate EMs. We propose an efficient bisection-based search heuristic to select a near-optimal mechanism from this ensemble. Empirical evaluation demonstrates that our method improves privacy (up to 5%) parameter ϵ over the conventional EM. Furthermore, its performance closely approximates the optimal DP solution under constraints on expected dissimilarity, input priors, and output evidence, offering a practical and scalable approach.

abstract_ts: As AI systems become more prevalent in elderly care, their form of embodiment, robot or screen-based, may influence user acceptance and engagement. This exploratory study examines how older adults interact with two distinct embodiments of an AI coaching system: the 2D avatar AnnSophie and the humanoid robot, Ameca. Through qualitative interviews with four participants, we examined perceptions of appearance, voice, usability, and integration potential in care settings. Participants generally accepted both systems but noted different strengths. Ann-Sophie was seen as visually pleasant and approachable, though some criticized its limited expressiveness and conversational depth. Ameca elicited stronger emotional responses: while appreciated for its realism and social cues, it occasionally provoked unease and prompted reflections on the role of robotics in caregiving. Across both systems, participants emphasized the importance of understandable language, personalization, and practical benefits. Our findings suggest that embodiment influences user experience, but does not solely determine acceptance. Context, expectation, and perceived usefulness were equally decisive. This points to the need for adaptive, context-aware AI agents and user-centered development strategies. Despite the limited number of participants, the study contributes to the ongoing debate on human-centered AI and provides insights for the design of socially acceptable digital coaching systems for the aging population.

abstract_ts: Micro/Nanomaterials are increasingly used in the biomedical field for advanced applications. In this work, microneedles made from biodegradable materials are designed and simulated for drug delivery systems. Different materials and dimensions are analyzed through stress and laminar flow studies to evaluate their performance. Biodegradable materials are chosen since they are safer for the environment and suitable for biomedical use. Stress analysis is carried out to study the effect of needle insertion on the skin, while flow analysis helps in understanding the velocity and pressure distribution of the drug. Additionally, micro/nano pumps are designed and simulated to handle small fluid volumes, ensuring controlled drug delivery. The pump uses piezoelectric actuation, and its performance is tested with variations in inlet, outlet, and channel sizes.

abstract_ts: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) face significant challenges in energy consumption optimization and network lifetime management, as conventional routing protocols often fail to balance energy efficiency with network connectivity and performance requirements. The lack of comprehensive comparative assessment of contemporary energy-efficient routing protocols hinders optimal protocol selection for specific WSN applications. This research presents a comparative simulation-based study of six prominent routing protocols: LEACH, Modified LEACH, TLEACH, E-DEEC, Multi-Chain PEGASIS, and MGEAR across various network infrastructures and application environments. Performance analysis employs realistic constraints including energy consumption patterns, network latency measurements, and active round counts to evaluate protocol performance. The comparative study reveals significant performance trade- offs between network connectivity and energy efficiency, with specific protocols demonstrating superior performance under different network conditions. This research provides quantitative performance metrics that enable network designers to make informed protocol selection decisions based on specific WSN constraints and requirements.

abstract_ts: The revolutionary effect of AI on smart civil infrastructure predictive maintenance is the focus of this research. Constantly monitoring structural health, detecting early indications of degradation, and preventing possible failures before they become dangerous or expensive occurrences are all possible thanks to infrastructure technologies that integrate artificial intelligence with smart sensors and predictive analytics. This study creates and assesses a state-of-the-art AI-powered predictive operation and failure patterns by analysing real-time data acquired from sensors implanted in urban infrastructure. The most relevant features are selected using the RFE technique, after data pretreatment has ensured clean and reliable input. A Gradient Boost module considerably enhances classification performance, and the suggested model, the Neural Decision Forest, integrates the best features of random forests and deep neural networks. In order to maximise learning efficiency, the design combines a convolutional autoencoder, fully linked layers, and a neural decision forest. The suggested GBCNN-AE model outperforms the competition, with experimental results showing a remarkable 97.37% accuracy across various cities information technology datasets. These results demonstrate the model's functionality, flexibility, and practicality, highlighting the possibility of AI-driven predictive maintenance to improve security, prolong the life of infrastructure, and encourage sustainable growth in contemporary smart cities.

abstract_ts: Cervical Cancer is still a major cause of cancer-related morbidity and mortality in women globally, especially in low and middle financial country where routine screening and diagnostic services are not easily accessible. Detecting the cervical cancer early is more important to improve outcomes of patient, minimizing treatment expenses, avoiding disease advancement. With the recent advances in computational intelligence, it has become possible to develop predictive models that can aid clinicians for the detection of cancer early and risk stratification of Cervical Cancer. The present study seeks to investigate application in Python-based ML algorithms in creating an effective predictive model for detecting cervical cancer. The designed system employs patient clinical information, demographic data, lifestyle data, and laboratory test results as the primary input features. Preprocessing methods like data cleaning, normalization, feature selection, and missing value handling are used to make the model reliable and accurate. Various machine learning classifiers like logistic regression, random forest, support vector machines (SVM), gradient boosting, and XGBoost are used and compared to determine the best algorithm for prediction. Each model's performance is measured with metrics like accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and area under the ROC curve (AUC-ROC). Bias reduction and generalizability improvement are facilitated through the use of cross-validation methods. The research not only seeks to determine the top-performing algorithm but also to interpret feature importance, thus providing insight into the most significant risk factors leading to the onset of cervical cancer. The use of Python-based machine learning platforms offers a cost-effective, scalable, and automated system which may be implemented in the clinical or non-clinical environment and is particularly viable in resource-limited areas. Finally, this predictive modelling is one which can aid healthcare practitioners to make timely data-driven decisions and enhance early detection levels to assist in the global fight against reducing the burden of cervical cancer.

abstract_ts: Monocular 3D human pose estimation (HPE) often encounters challenges such as depth ambiguity and occlusion during the 2D-to-3D lifting process. Additionally, traditional methods may overlook multi-scale skeleton features when utilizing skeleton structure information, which can negatively impact the accuracy of pose estimation. To address these challenges, this paper introduces a novel 3D pose estimation method, HyperDiff, which integrates diffusion models with HyperGCN. The diffusion model effectively captures data uncertainty, alleviating depth ambiguity and occlusion. Meanwhile, HyperGCN, serving as a denoiser, employs multi-granularity structures to accurately model high-order correlations between joints. This improves the model’s denoising capability especially for complex poses. Experimental results demonstrate that HyperDiff achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Human3.6M and MPI-INF-3DHP datasets and can flexibly adapt to varying computational resources to balance performance and efficiency. Code is released at https://github.com/IHENL/HyperDiff

abstract_ts: We introduce Music Source Restoration (MSR), a novel task addressing the gap between idealized source separation and real-world music production. Current Music Source Separation (MSS) approaches assume mixtures are simple sums of sources, ignoring signal degradations employed during music production like equalization, compression, and reverb. MSR models mixtures as degraded sums of individually degraded sources, with the goal of recovering original, undegraded signals. Due to the lack of data for MSR, we present RawStems, a dataset annotation of 578 songs with unprocessed source signals organized into 8 primary and 17 secondary instrument groups, totaling 354.13 hours. To the best of our knowledge, RawStems is the first dataset that contains unprocessed music stems with hierarchical categories. We consider spectral filtering, dynamic range compression, harmonic distortion, reverb and lossy codec as possible degradations, and establish U-Former as a baseline method, demonstrating the feasibility of MSR on our dataset. We release the RawStems dataset annotations, degradation simulation pipeline, training code and pre-trained models to be publicly available.

abstract_ts: The Middle-Income Trap (MIT) refers to the situation where a country’s development prospects may diverge after reaching the middle-income stage (with a per capita GDP of approximately USD4000). The outcomes could be either continued development toward becoming a developed country or economic stagnation leading to the MIT. Therefore, studying the MIT is crucial. Practically, it affects the future of many developing economies and consequently the global economic outlook. Theoretically, existing literature lacks in-depth research on the MIT and fails to provide a theoretical model or scenario analysis. After over 40 years of rapid growth, China’s per capita GDP continuously exceeded USD10,000 in 2019–2020, nearing the standard of high-income countries. China has set a significant goal of reaching a moderately developed country status by 2035, providing a solid subject for theoretical research. This chapter will construct a theoretical model of the MIT based on its connotation and international experiences, reveal possible scenarios and conditions theoretically, and conduct empirical analysis to offer references for China and other economies to smoothly pass through the MIT and advance into the high-income country club.

abstract_ts: Brassica oleracea variety “italica” (broccoli) is a commercially important vegetable crop within the Brassicaceae family since it has been cultivated for over two millennia after originating from the Mediterranean region. Broccoli furthers sustainable agriculture beyond economic and nutritional importance, and glucosinolates in broccoli improve soil fertility and likewise stimulate helpful microbial activity furthermore provide natural pest regulation. Regardless of its numerous merits, biotic stressors severely constrain its production because downy mildew, club root, fusarium wilt, and turnip mosaic virus do collectively cause substantial yield losses. This chapter takes a look in depth at the disease cycles of major pathogens, it analyzes all of the dynamics of host pathogen interactions, and it explores each of the underlying genetic mechanisms of resistance. Particular emphasis goes toward resistant cultivars’ development and performance. Rapid evolution of the pathogen population generates innate challenges, specifically in pathotype-specific and broad-spectrum cultivars that compromise resistance durability. Furthermore, the chapter explores the application of biotechnological strategies (expression of cry genes in plants for pest resistance), while it addresses the emerging issue of pest tolerance to interventions. Also highlighted is the changing role of genomic databases like BRAD, ensemble plants, NGDC, plant garden in resistance gene discovery, synteny analysis, comparative genomics, and molecular marker development. Along with next-generation sequencing as well as advanced analytical tools, these resources have substantially strengthened broccoli breeding programs so that these programs can precisely target genetic improvements. This work outlines a thorough framework for improving resilience of broccoli against biotic stresses and securing its role in advancing sustainable agriculture since conventional breeding, genomic technologies, and management practices become integrated.

abstract_ts: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a neurodevelopmental disorder that is defined by persistent social interaction and communication difficulties, along with limited interests, repetitive activities, and sensory processing differences. ASD individuals tend to have distinct patterns of recognizing, conveying, and reacting to emotions. This paper provides a comparative evaluation of two deep learning methods to facial emotion recognition in autistic children: an adapted DenseNet121 model and a new Hybrid Attention-based Explainable Network (HAXN). Tested on a challenging dataset of 833 facial images for six emotions with extreme class imbalance, our experiments show that although DenseNet121 attains greater overall accuracy (65.33% vs. 61.33%), HAXN exhibits better generalization (72.22% validation accuracy) and offers key model interpretability through built-in attention mechanisms. Both models perform well on the majority joy class (F1 > 0.86) but fail with minority emotions, especially fear (0% F1-score in HAXN). Main conclusions are: (1) traditional CNNs (e.g., DenseNet121) are the current champions on imbalanced emotion classification tasks, over attention-based variants; (2) attention mechanisms allow for clinically actionable model transparency; and (3) extreme class imbalance is still the main bottleneck for autismspecific emotion recognition. This work opens doors to assistive technologies that make trade-offs on accuracy and explainability, and underscores pressing requirements for future minority-class learning work.
